figures.name: Modu Chanyu
figures.significance: United the steppe tribes; template for all subsequent steppe empires

figures.name: Bumin Qaghan
figures.significance: First use of the title Khagan; founded the first explicitly Turkic empire

figures.name: Bilge Qaghan
figures.significance: Documented in the Orkhon inscriptions — the oldest known Turkic texts

name: Uyghur Khaganate
note: One of history's few Manichaean states; adopted Manichaeism as state religion after 762 — a major civilizational break from Göktürk Tengriism

figures.name: Bayanchur Khan
figures.significance: Founded the Uyghur Khaganate; adopted Manichaeism as state religion

name: Khazar Khaganate
note: Ruling class converted to Judaism — a unique case; possibly a diplomatic choice to remain neutral between the Christian Byzantine and Muslim Abbasid spheres

figures.name: Bulan
figures.significance: The Khazar ruler who converted to Judaism

name: Mongol Empire
note: Religiously tolerant by policy (Yasa law); the different successor khanates each converted to the local dominant religion

policies.name: Yasa — Religious Tolerance Policy
policies.description: Mongol legal code mandated religious tolerance. Policy — each successor khanate converted to local religions independently.

figures.name: Genghis Khan
figures.significance: Greatest conqueror by territory in history; created the Yasa and the meritocratic military system

figures.name: Ogedei Khan
figures.significance: Completed the conquest of North China; launched the invasion of Europe

name: Golden Horde
note: Converted to Sunni Islam ~1313 under Özbeg Khan; Mongol rulers gradually Turkified — adopted Kipchak Turkic as the dominant language

figures.name: Batu Khan
figures.significance: Conquered Russia and Eastern Europe; founded the Golden Horde

figures.name: Ozbeg Khan
figures.significance: Converted the Golden Horde to Sunni Islam (1313); modern Uzbeks are named after him

name: Chagatai Khanate
note: Chagatai Turkic became the prestige literary language of Central Asia after Mongol Turkification

figures.name: Chagatai Khan
figures.significance: Second son of Genghis Khan; Chagatai Turkic language is named after him
